response: {"calibration_score":0.15,"overconfident_count":5,"underconfident_count":0,"well_calibrated_count":7,"biases_detected":["path_assumption_bias: repeatedly assumed file paths and CWDs were correct without verification, leading to failed Bash/Read/Grep calls in units bc59cf34-20 and bc59cf34-22","environment_state_assumption: assumed services were running on expected ports without first checking pm2/lsof, causing multiple curl failures in bc59cf34-20 and bc59cf34-27","tool_sequence_anchoring: in bc59cf34-8, anchored on a heredoc-based jq script approach despite repeated failures, requiring 3 Write attempts before switching strategy","optimistic_success_bias: in c6ea2b0e-8, issued Edit without verifying exact string match, resulting in initial failure and requiring Read-then-reattempt cycle","complexity_underestimation: bc59cf34-8 jq aggregator took significantly more iterations than the approach complexity implied, suggesting underestimation of jq heredoc/encoding edge cases"],"recommendations":["verify CWD and key env vars at unit start before issuing path-dependent Bash commands — a single pwd + ls check prevents the majority of observed failures","use the Write tool for multi-line scripts rather than heredoc in Bash; heredoc quoting and encoding issues caused 3+ retries in bc59cf34-8","before assuming services are available, run pm2 list and lsof -iTCP:PORT -sTCP:LISTEN as a preflight rather than discovering failures mid-task","for Edit operations on large files, always Read the target region first to confirm the exact string before issuing the edit; would have prevented the c6ea2b0e-8 initial failure","when monitoring background test runs, avoid sleep-based polling in Bash; use the TaskOutput or Monitor tool directly — the sleep 60 call in bc59cf34-24 failed and wasted a tool round-trip"]}
